title: Pull and analyze journalctl logs for all 4 clunger.service crashes in the last 24h

log.message: Created from Congress #62 verdict on: [idea]: service clunger.service restarted 4x in last 24h — possible flakiness (GitHub issue: https:/

Source verdict: Four restarts in 24 hours is a pattern, not noise — this needs root-cause investigation before any fix is applied. The danger here is someone slapping a restart loop or watchdog on it and calling it s...

Analyzed clunger restarts: NOT 4 restarts but ~60+ in 24h. Found 3 distinct crash categories: (1) 3x exit-code=1 crashes at 17:54-17:55 UTC Mar 25 — Anthropic API returning HTTP 400 invalid_request_error killing uncaught promise rejections before global handler was added; (2) 2x OOM-kill at 01:39 and 04:23 UTC Mar 26 — process killed by kernel OOM; (3) All other stops were intentional deploys correlating exactly with git commits. The 400 crashes and OOM kills are real flakiness. OOM at 04:23 had 316MB peak — may be congress session memory leak.

Investigation complete. Issue #53 kept open — 5 genuine crashes found (3x API 400 unhandled promise kills pre-global-handler, 2x OOM kills including one at 316MB peak). Commented findings at https://github.com/bigclungus/bigclungus-meta/issues/53#issuecomment-4132935559. 400 crashes already fixed. OOM needs investigation.
